sections.paragraph: m. pl. cookings, pieces cooked, from the root אָפָה وفى to cook, of the form תּוּגָה, תּוּשִׁיָּה, with נ formative, like קָצִין from קָצָה. It occurs once in a very difficult passage, Lev 6:14, of a cake for sacrifice, “thou shalt offer it fried תֻּפִינֵי מִנְחַת פִּתִּים as cookings of meat offerings in pieces,” i.e. cooked in the manner of a meat offering in pieces (compare Lev 2:4 7:9 ), from the vulgar idiom of the language; as to which, see Lehrg. p. 810.
